whenToUse.summary: All products sit together like window shopping, with each product linking into its OWN dedicated sales funnel rather than a generic product page or shared cart. Mimics a traditional ecommerce site but routes every visitor into a proper funnel. Use when a client has multiple products/offers and wants one branded hub where each item still gets the full funnel treatment.
goals: Present multiple products/offers in one branded gallery
Route every product click into its own dedicated funnel (no shared cart)
Give an ecommerce 'shop' feel while preserving funnel conversion
Organize offers along the value ladder for browsing buyers
signals: Client has multiple distinct products/offers to show together
Wants a 'shop'/'store' feel but with funnels, not a cart
Needs a hub to link out to several individual funnels
Browsing buyers who want to choose among offers
antiSignals: Single product/offer -> use that product's VSL or Sales Letter Funnel directly
Brand-hub website with blog/PR/social -> Funnel Hub (other group)
Free/just-shipping single front end -> 2-Step Tripwire / Book Funnel
Recurring subscription is the only offer -> Membership/Continuity

pageStructure.purpose: Display all products together as browsable cards (window shopping). Each product card links OUT to its own dedicated funnel (VSL or Sales Letter), never to a shared cart.
keyElements: Grid of PRODUCT CARDS: image, name, one-line benefit, price/anchor
Each card CTA links to that product's dedicated funnel
Brand signature story / trust band (testimonials, guarantees)
No shared cart / no generic add-to-cart
widgets: Product cards with outbound links to each product's funnel (custom HTML grid, or Vercel-built gallery embedded)
Skill 44: optional opt-in/newsletter form in a footer band
Skill 44: tags on click-through if tracking which product is browsed

pageStructure.purpose: Each product card lands the visitor in its OWN funnel built from a buyer-group template (VSL Funnel or Sales Letter Funnel), with that funnel's order form, bump, and OTOs.
keyElements: Built from video-sales-letter-vsl or sales-letter template
Own order form + bump + OTO/downsell + confirmation
widgets: Reuses the linked template's Skill 44 widgets (order form, bump, upsell)

primaryPersona.script: Brand architecture across the storefront — consistent brand voice, Signature Story, Be/Do/Have depth
primaryPersona.role: Governs the storefront's brand consistency so all product cards feel like one coherent brand

salesScript.script: StoryBrand SB7 per product card (compressed) — customer as Hero, product as the Plan
salesScript.why: Each card is a micro-narrative: it must make the customer the hero and the product the clear next step, in one line, so the browser clicks into the right funnel.

supportingPersonas.script: SB7 one-liner / BrandScript per product card
supportingPersonas.role: Writes each product card as a clear customer-as-hero micro-narrative

supportingPersonas.script: Product-card microcopy — benefit-first names, one-line hooks, button copy
supportingPersonas.role: Writes the tight card headlines and CTAs

supportingPersonas.script: Value Ladder ordering of products (ascending value)
supportingPersonas.role: Arranges the storefront so browsing buyers see the ladder, low to high

copyFramework.notes: Brand Mapping governs the storefront's coherent brand voice; StoryBrand writes each product card as a customer-as-hero micro-narrative; Copywriting Secrets tightens the card microcopy; Blackbook orders the products along the value ladder. The deep selling copy lives in each linked funnel (VSL or Sales Letter template), not on the storefront itself.

ghlBuild.funnelType: GHL Funnel or Website (1 gallery page) + N separate product funnels
skill6Steps: Create Funnel/Website -> name with client prefix -> Create
Build Step/Page 1 = 'Storefront Gallery Page' (product-card grid with outbound links)
For each product, build a SEPARATE funnel from the VSL or Sales Letter buyer template and link its card to it
skill44Widgets: Optional opt-in/newsletter form on the gallery footer
Each linked product funnel uses its own template's Skill 44 widgets (order form, bump, one-click upsell)
products: No shared cart on the storefront; each product is sold in its own funnel via that funnel's GHL Payments product
ghlBuild.notes: The storefront is a hub: build the gallery as a GHL page (custom HTML grid in the code block, or a Vercel-built gallery pulled in via embed link) whose product-card buttons link OUT to each product's own funnel. Do NOT wire a shared cart. Build each product funnel separately from the buyer-group VSL or Sales Letter template. Pre-create any opt-in form/tags via Skill 44 (or GHL MCP/API). Store product images in GHL media storage via Skill 44/GHL MCP/API.
